title: Saturn–Neptune Conjunction
description: Saturn and Neptune appear close together in the evening sky. Saturn is easily visible to the naked eye, but Neptune is very faint and requires binoculars or a telescope.

title: Crescent Moon near Mercury
description: A thin crescent Moon appears close to Mercury low in the evening twilight. A clear western horizon is essential; binoculars may help.

title: Moon close to Saturn
description: The crescent Moon passes close to Saturn in the evening sky, creating a striking and easy naked-eye pairing.

title: Venus–Mercury Conjunction (Low Twilight)
description: Venus and Mercury appear close together very low in the twilight sky. Visibility depends strongly on latitude and horizon conditions.

title: Six-Planet Morning Line-up
source: Compiled from NASA, In-The-Sky.org, Star Walk
description: Several planets — including Mercury, Venus, Mars, Jupiter, and Saturn — will be visible along the ecliptic before sunrise. Neptune is also present but requires binoculars or a telescope. This is a visual line-up rather than a precise planetary alignment.

title: Venus–Neptune Close Conjunction
description: Venus passes extremely close to faint Neptune in the evening sky. Venus is brilliant, but Neptune requires binoculars or a telescope.

title: Venus–Saturn Conjunction
description: Bright Venus and golden Saturn appear close together in the evening sky, forming a striking naked-eye pairing.

title: Venus–Jupiter Conjunction (Evening)
description: Brilliant Venus and Jupiter appear very close together in the evening sky, creating one of the most eye-catching planetary pairings of the year. Both planets are easily visible to the naked eye shortly after sunset, shining low above the western horizon.

title: Mars–Jupiter Conjunction
description: Reddish Mars and bright Jupiter pass close together in the night sky. The contrast between Jupiter’s steady brilliance and Mars’ warm orange glow makes this conjunction especially striking. Both planets are easily visible to the naked eye.

title: Crescent Moon meets Venus
description: A slender crescent Moon appears beside dazzling Venus in the evening sky. This elegant pairing is easy to spot with the naked eye and is often considered one of the most beautiful celestial sights of the year.
